structured_payload_candidate.title: Adjustability of Setpoints, Timers, Deadbands, PID Gains

structured_payload_candidate.title: Override Capability for Points

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarm Levels Definition

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Mode Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operators shall have the ability to put any device in/out of maintenance mode. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed, except life safety alarms. Issue a daily Level 3 alarm if device remains in maintenance mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress all alarms except life safety; issue daily Level 3 alarm at scheduled time indicating device still in maintenance mode.

If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VFD Speed Point Configuration
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The speed command point for VFDs should be configured so that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% corresponds to maximum speed set in the VFD, not necessarily 60 Hz.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Configure speed command so 0% = 0 Hz, 100% = maximum speed in VFD.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: To avoid operator confusion, the speed command point (and speed feedback point, if used) for VFDs should be configured so that a speed of 0% corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% corresponds to maximum speed set in the VFD, not necessarily 60 Hz.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Lag Alternation Based on Staging Runtime
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Identical parallel staged equipment (e.g., CHW pumps, cooling towers)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is made last stage and the one with the least hours is made lead stage.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: More than one equipment off or more than one on
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/lag based on Staging Runtime: most hours to last stage, least hours to lead stage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Alternation for Redundant Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is made last stage and the one with the least hours is made earlier stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be TRUE when the system is proven ON, achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes, and ready and able to serve its load.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Selective Alarm Suppression by System Type
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system, suppress only high-temperature alarms from loads. If FALSE for heating water system, suppress only low-temperature alarms. If FALSE for air-side system, suppress all alarms from loads.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress specific alarm types based on source system type.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 4.4°C for 5 minutes, send two HHW plant requests, override OA damper to minimum, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT at least 6°C. Disable when SAT rises above 7°C for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If the supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Fan Staging - Stage Up
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-Zone VAV Air-Handling Unit with relief fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When control loop is above minimum speed plus 15%, start stage-up timer. Each time timer reaches 7 minutes, start next relief fan and reset timer. Timer resets and freezes if loop drops below threshold.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Control loop output above minimum speed plus 15%.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Stage Up. When control loop is above minimum speed plus 15%, start stage-up timer. Each time the timer reaches 7 minutes, start the next relief fan (and open the associated damper) in the relief system group, per staging order, and reset the timer to 0. The timer is reset to 0 and frozen if control loop is below minimum speed plus 15%.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Stage Up. When control loop is above minimum speed plus 15%, start stage-up timer. Each time the timer reaches 7 minutes, start the next relief fan (and open the associated damper) in the relief system group, per staging order, and reset the timer to 0. The timer is reset to 0 and frozen if control loop is below minimum speed plus 15%.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: Stage Up. When control loop is above minimum speed plus 15%, start stage-up timer. structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Fan Staging - Stage Down
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-Zone VAV Air-Handling Unit with relief fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When PID loop is below minimum speed, start stage-down timer. Each time timer reaches 5 minutes, shut off lag fan and reset timer. Timer resets and freezes if loop rises above minimum speed or all fans off.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: PID loop output below minimum speed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Stage Down. When PID loop is below minimum speed, start stage-down timer. Each time the timer reaches 5 minutes, shut off lag fan per staging order and reset the timer to 0. The timer is reset to 0 and frozen if PID loop rises above minimum speed or all fans are OFF. If all fans are OFF, go to Stage 0 (all dampers open and all fans OFF).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Stage Down. When PID loop is below minimum speed, start stage-down timer. Each time the timer reaches 5 minutes, shut off lag fan per staging order and reset the timer to 0. The timer is reset to 0 and frozen if PID loop rises above minimum speed or all fans are OFF. If all fans are OFF, go to Stage 0 (all dampers open and all fans OFF).

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: Stage Down. When PID loop is below minimum speed, start stage-down timer. Each time the timer reaches 5 minutes, shut off lag fan per staging order and reset the timer to 0. The timer is reset to 0 and frozen if PID loop rises above minimum speed or all fans are OFF. If all fans are OFF, go to Stage 0 (all dampers open and all fans OFF).
candidate_entries.evidence_text: Stage Down. When PID loop is below minimum speed, start stage-down timer. structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two or more heating hot-water plant requests, override OA damper to minimum position, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send heating plant requests, set OA damper to minimum, modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C; disable when SAT > 7°C for 5 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
candidate_entries.evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Operational sequence: freeze protection stage 1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed Reset in Heating and Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan speed is reset based on heating or cooling loop signal: from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed for 100% to 50% heating, MinSpeed for 50% to 0% heating and deadband, MinSpeed for 0% to 25% cooling, reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed for 25% to 50% cooling, MedSpeed for 50% to 75% cooling, and reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed for 75% to 100% cooling.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating or cooling loop signal value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset fan speed according to the specified ranges
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed. e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed. f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed. g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed. f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed. g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
candidate_entries.evidence_text: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. Else if the coil pump speed is less than 95%, send 0 Requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling-loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map cooling-loop output to active primary airflow setpoint between minimum and cooling maximum endpoints. If AHU SAT > room temperature, limit setpoint to minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Control Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send requests for cooling SAT reset based on zone temperature exceeding cooling setpoint or cooling loop output. If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. If by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. If cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature or cooling loop conditions
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 3, 2, 1, or 0 requests as per conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
